text: {"title": "Deep GraphRAG: A Balanced Approach to Hierarchical Retrieval and Adaptive Integration", "tags": ["GraphRAG", "RAG", "Knowledge Graph", "Reinforcement Learning", "LLM"], "sections": {"metadata": "arXiv 2601.11144 (cs.IR, cs.AI), Ant Group + Zhejiang University. A GraphRAG framework balancing global breadth and local efficiency.", "core_contribution": "Proposes Deep GraphRAG: a hierarchical global-to-local retrieval strategy with dynamic re-ranking, trained via DW-GRPO so a compact 1.5B LLM approaches 70B-level knowledge integration.", "background": "Existing GraphRAG systems trade off global comprehensiveness against local efficiency; neither extreme serves multi-hop questions well.", "method": "A two-stage retriever: global subgraph selection then adaptive local expansion, with a learned re-ranker. DW-GRPO (distribution-weighted GRPO) trains the integration policy.", "experiments": "Evaluated on multi-hop QA benchmarks against HippoRAG and vanilla GraphRAG baselines; reports gains in answer accuracy and retrieval precision.", "key_insights": "A compact 1.5B model, given balanced hierarchical context, matches a 70B model's integration quality — context shape beats raw scale here.", "reproduction": "pip install the released package; prepare the knowledge graph; run the provided training script with DW-GRPO config, then evaluate with the bundled harness.", "limitations": "Single-domain evaluation; the re-ranker adds latency; DW-GRPO training stability needs more study across domains.", "related_work": "Builds on GraphRAG, HippoRAG, and GRPO-style RL post-training; contrasts with flat dense retrieval.", "personal_notes": "Use this when the corpus has genuine graph structure and multi-hop questions dominate; overkill for flat FAQ retrieval."}}
